mission.title: cilium: ENI IPAM mode should add support for assigning IP prefixes to EC2 instance
mission.description: ENI IPAM mode should add support for assigning IP prefixes to EC2 instance. Requested by 26+ users.

steps.title: Check current cilium deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cilium version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
helm list -n cilium 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cilium installation.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
## Proposal / RFE

AWS now supports assigning IP prefixes to EC2 instance. ENI IPAM mode in Cilium should also add support this as this allows increasing the pod density. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-prefix-eni.html

steps.title: Apply the fix for ENI IPAM mode should add support for assigning IP prefixes…
steps.description:
AWS introduced support for assigning prefixes to EC2 network interfaces - [ prefix delegation (pd) ](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-prefix-eni.html). Some of the benefits of using PD are:
* Increased pod density on nodes (~ 16x more pods)
* Reduced reliance on operator for pod IP allocation.
* Reduced API calls to AWS and faster pod startup time.
* Reduced cost in Amazon VPC IP Address Manager https://github.com/cilium/cilium/issues/16987#issuecomment-1006225191

With

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "ENI IPAM mode should add support for assigning IP prefixes…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
AWS introduced support for assigning prefixes to EC2 network interfaces - [ prefix delegation (pd) ](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/ec2-prefix-eni.html). Some of the benefits of using PD are:
* Increased pod density on nodes (~ 16x more pods)
* Reduced reliance on operator for pod IP allocation.
* Reduced API calls to AWS and faster pod startup time.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
